name: flutter-verify
description: Runs comprehensive verification checks to ensure code quality before committing changes

purpose.overview: Executes a complete verification loop that checks your Flutter project for code quality, test coverage, build integrity, security vulnerabilities, and accessibility compliance. Provides a single entry point to validate that your code meets quality standards before committing or deploying.
useCases: As part of a pre-deployment checklist
To get a comprehensive quality assessment

skipTests.description: Skip test execution and coverage checks (useful for quick analysis-only runs)

verbose.description: Show detailed output for each verification step

projectPath.description: Absolute path to the Flutter project to analyze. If not provided, uses the current workspace directory.

steps.name: Environment Detection
steps.description: Detects Flutter/FVM setup (checks for .fvm directory or .fvmrc, determines if commands should use 'fvm flutter' or 'flutter')

steps.name: Static Analysis
steps.description: Runs flutter analyze to check for code issues, warnings, and style violations

steps.name: Test Execution
steps.description: Runs flutter test --coverage to execute all tests and generate coverage data

steps.name: Coverage Check
steps.description: Parses coverage data and compares against thresholds (80% overall, 90% critical paths, 95% business logic)

steps.name: Build Verification
steps.description: Executes flutter build in debug mode to verify the project compiles successfully

steps.name: Security Scan
steps.description: Scans codebase for hardcoded secrets, insecure storage, missing input validation, and OWASP Mobile Top 10 vulnerabilities

steps.name: Accessibility Check
steps.description: Verifies semantic labels, touch target sizes (minimum 48dp), and color contrast ratios (WCAG AA 4.5:1)

steps.name: Report Generation
steps.description: Compiles all check results into a comprehensive report with pass/fail status and action items

sections: Environment (Flutter version, Dart version, FVM status, command used)
Coverage (overall, critical paths, business logic percentages)
Action Items (prioritized by severity: CRITICAL, HIGH, MEDIUM)

errorHandling.cause: Code contains syntax errors, type errors, or severe linting violations
solution: Review error messages from flutter analyze
Import missing dependencies
Fix type mismatches
Remove unused imports
Address null safety issues

errorHandling.cause: One or more tests are failing
solution: Review test failure messages
Check for logic errors in implementation
Verify test expectations are correct
Check for missing test setup or teardown
Debug async timing issues
Run failing tests individually: flutter test path/to/test_file.dart

errorHandling.cause: Test coverage is below required thresholds
solution: Add tests for uncovered code paths
Focus on business logic first (highest threshold)
Then critical paths (user flows, error handling)
Finally overall coverage
Use coverage visualization: genhtml coverage/lcov.info -o coverage/html
Review build error messages
Run flutter pub get to resolve dependencies
Resolve version conflicts in pubspec.yaml
Run flutter pub run build_runner build if code generation needed
Check platform-specific configuration files
Verify all assets are declared in pubspec.yaml
Consider using Flutter Build Resolver assistant
CRITICAL: Hardcoded secrets → Move to environment variables or secure storage
HIGH: Missing input validation → Add validator functions to forms
MEDIUM: Insecure storage → Use flutter_secure_storage for sensitive data
LOW: Missing certificate pinning → Implement for production API calls
Run /flutter-security for detailed audit
Missing semantics → Wrap widgets with Semantics(label: 'description')
Small touch targets → Ensure interactive elements are at least 48x48dp
Poor contrast → Adjust colors to meet WCAG AA standard (4.5:1 ratio)
Test with screen readers (TalkBack on Android, VoiceOver on iOS)

relatedCommands.description: Create an implementation plan before starting development
Save a checkpoint after verification passes
Run a detailed security audit with OWASP Mobile Top 10 checks
Coordinate multiple assistants for complex quality improvements

relatedAssistants.description: Helps fix build errors with minimal code changes
Guides test-driven development to improve coverage
Provides architectural guidance to improve code quality

requirements: Requirement 1.1: Execute static analysis using flutter analyze
Requirement 1.2: Execute all tests using flutter test with coverage enabled
Requirement 1.3: Calculate coverage percentage and compare against thresholds
Requirement 1.4: Execute a debug build using flutter build
Requirement 1.5: Scan codebase for hardcoded secrets and security vulnerabilities
Requirement 1.6: Check for accessibility compliance including semantic labels and touch target sizes
Requirement 1.7: Generate a verification report showing pass/fail status for each check with actionable recommendations
Requirement 1.8: Provide specific error details and remediation steps for failed checks
notes: Verification loop executes checks sequentially. If a critical check fails (e.g., static analysis or build), subsequent checks may be skipped to save time
Coverage thresholds are configurable but default to: 80% overall, 90% critical paths, 95% business logic
Security scanning uses regex patterns to detect common secret formats. False positives may occur for test data or comments
Accessibility checks are static analysis only. Manual testing with assistive technologies is still recommended
For CI/CD integration, this command can be run as a pre-commit hook or in a build pipeline
Use --verbose flag when debugging verification failures to see detailed output from each check
Overall score is calculated as (passed checks / total checks) × 100%
